content:
authorized to exercise the functions of Yang di-Pertuan Agong
2. (1) There shall be paid to the Timbalan Yang di-Pertuan Agong
or other Ruler authorized by law to exercise the functions of the
Yang di-Pertuan Agong during any period during which he exercises
the functions of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and is not receiving
the emoluments payable to him as the Ruler of his State the
monthly sum of eighteen thousand ringgit:
Provided that in relation to a Ruler aforesaid whose emolument
payable to him as the Ruler of his State is a monthly sum in excess
of eighteen thousand ringgit, the reference to “eighteen thousand
ringgit” appearing immediately after the words “monthly sum of”
shall be construed as a reference to the total of the sum of eighteen
thousand ringgit and the excess.
(2) The monthly sum payable under subsection (1) shall—
(a) accrue from day to day; and
(b) be payable on the last day of each month or on such other
day as the Minister of Finance may from time to time
determine.
6
Laws of Malaysia
ACT 374
(3) In the event of there being a change of the Ruler exercising
the functions of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, there shall be made
such adjustment and distribution of the monthly payment payable
under subsection (1) as the circumstances of the case may require.
Entertainment

Entertainment
3. (1) There shall be paid to the Comptroller of the Household in
respect of any period during which the Timbalan Yang di-Pertuan
Agong or other Ruler exercises the functions of the Yang di-
Pertuan Agong the monthly sum of three thousand ringgit for
entertainment.
(2) The monthly sum payable under subsection (1) shall accrue
and be payable in the same manner as the sum payable under
subsection 2(1).
Other privileges
4.
The Timbalan Yang di-Pertuan Agong or other Ruler exercising
the functions of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong shall be entitled to the
privileges specified in the Schedule.
Timbalan Yang Di-Pertuan Agong (Remuneration)
7
SCHEDULE
[Section 4]
1.
Residence
Furnished residence with staff shall be provided and maintained by the
Government.
2.
Transport and Traveling
Two motorcars shall be provided and maintained by the Government and
all traveling expenses paid by the Government.
3.
Medical Facilities
Medical attention and hospital treatment shall be provided by the Government.
8
